sourceMovie.title: Deep Blue Sea
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Deep Blue Sea (1999) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Meg
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Deep Blue Sea clicked for you, The Meg (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Deep Blue Sea clicked for you, Resident Evil (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Deep Blue Sea?
faq.answer: Try The Meg, Resident Evil, Meg 2: The Trench—each shares DNA with Deep Blue Sea while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Deep Blue Sea?
faq.answer: Deep Blue Sea (1999) is best described as Action, Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Deep Blue Sea part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Deep Blue Sea Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
